hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions by mutating position 5 to aromatic residues and position 11 to hydrophobic residues will improve selectivity margin without compromising SSTR2 binding affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be improved by disrupting conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. The next iteration will focus on these positions with guided mutations to increase Δmargin beyond the native SST-14 level.

proposed_changes.rationale: Target non-pharmacophore positions known to contribute to off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5 conserved pocket contacts). Mutating these positions can disrupt off-target interactions without affecting the pharmacophore core.

Propose mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. These mutations are likely to reduce off-target binding while preserving the integrity of the pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
